authoritative_sources.title: Electric and Hybrid Vehicles: Battery, Charging & Safety
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the primary U.S. vehicle-safety regulator. This public EV safety page is authoritative for high-voltage battery safety framing, propulsion-battery requirements, and the expectation that service should be handled by qualified technicians with EV-specific training.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: EV intake safety grounding and technician-qualification boundary
used_for: refusal to improvise unsafe service handling

authoritative_sources.title: Interim Guidance for Electric and Hybrid-Electric Vehicles Equipped With High Voltage Batteries - Towing and Recovery Operators and Vehicle Storage Facilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's interim EV guidance is an authoritative operating reference for damaged or suspect EVs, including energized-battery assumptions, delayed-fire risk, storage-distance precautions, and the instruction to involve authorized service centers instead of making informal release decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety hold, damaged-vehicle quarantine, and release-boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Resources Related to Investigations and Recalls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This NHTSA page is authoritative for recall workflow boundaries because it centralizes VIN recall lookup, investigations, complaints, and manufacturer communications inside the federal recall program.

authoritative_sources.title: Manufacturer Communications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal early-warning rules require manufacturers to submit bulletins, warranty and policy extensions, and other communications to NHTSA, making this the authoritative public source for official service and warranty communications tied to a vehicle or equipment issue.
used_for: conflict handling when tenant guidance diverges from official communications

authoritative_sources.title: VIN Decoder
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's VIN decoder is the authoritative public reference for manufacturer-reported vehicle identity, plant, and build information, which is a prerequisite for trustworthy EV service, claim, and component traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: vehicle identity confirmation and case grounding
used_for: authoritative vehicle grounding before claim work

authoritative_sources.title: Global MMOG/LE Implementation Training - 6th Edition
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is a recognized automotive-industry standards body. Its MMOG/LE framework is presented as the universal standard for automotive supply-chain management processes, which makes it authoritative for materials, supplier, and inventory-control discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: parts availability, supplier dependency, and inventory-control review
used_for: escalation when parts flow depends on external approval

authoritative_sources.title: B-16 | Global Transport Label Standard for the Automotive Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's B-16 standard is a recognized automotive supply-chain standard for transport label design and data exchange and is authoritative for shipment and return traceability expectations at supplier and logistics handoff points.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: replacement-part receipt and removed-battery shipment handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Automotive Warranty Management Key Terms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's public warranty terminology guide is authoritative for automotive warranty vocabulary such as product service manual, KPI, PDCA, PPAP, and supplier quality assurance, which grounds the lane in automotive warranty language instead of generic service wording.

authoritative_sources.title: What Is The Warranty On My Ford Hybrid Or Electric Vehicle Battery?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Ford's public warranty page is authoritative for Ford EV battery coverage and gives current operational detail on covered defects, capacity-loss thresholds, and certified-dealer service expectations for Ford EV battery cases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: coverage verification and dealer-service boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Bulletin No. PIE0753F - High Voltage Battery Pack Replacement
authoritative_sources.publisher: General Motors via NHTSA Manufacturer Communications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This GM bulletin is an official manufacturer communication filed with NHTSA. It is authoritative for the vehicles named in the bulletin and provides concrete steps for verifying warranty coverage, starting a TAC case, ordering through the battery service center, and handling dangerous-goods constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: battery replacement authorization, parts ordering, and return preparation

authoritative_sources.title: Technical Service Bulletin 25-EV-002H - HV Battery Inspection & Replacement
authoritative_sources.publisher: Hyundai Motor America via NHTSA Manufacturer Communications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Hyundai bulletin is an official manufacturer communication filed with NHTSA. It is authoritative for the covered IONIQ vehicles and gives concrete dealer workflow details on active-DTC checks, applicable VIN windows, certified-dealer limits, and parts information for battery replacement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: diagnostic-gated repair intake and parts verification

workflow_stages.name: EV intake and authoritative vehicle grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, dealership, VIN identity, model applicability, open recall or campaign status, and whether the request is in scope before normal service execution starts.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety prerequisite and qualified-service validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify high-voltage safety posture, authorized or certified repair eligibility, and whether the case must move to hold before diagnosis or repair continues.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Diagnosis, coverage, and authorization progression
workflow_stages.description: Use DTC evidence, warranty terms, campaign applicability, and OEM bulletin conditions to decide whether the case can move into approved service, remain blocked, or require escalation.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Parts, battery, and return-flow coordination
workflow_stages.description: Track replacement-part ordering, battery service center routing, transport-label expectations, and removed-battery return steps while keeping supplier and logistics authority explicit.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Hold, rework, and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Move the case into hold, rework, or escalation when recall, quality, battery safety, traceability, or supplier-approval issues appear and stop short of final release authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Only close the case when post-repair validation, claim or policy evidence, return or shipping artifacts when required, and authoritative closure records are present.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: repair orders, appointment records, service advisor notes, and DMS case histories
VIN decode results, open recall or campaign status, and manufacturer communication or bulletin references
warranty coverage pages, claim drafts, policy-extension records, and OEM portal status results
scan-tool outputs, active DTC snapshots, battery system assembly identifiers, and post-repair validation records
parts orders, battery service center instructions, return authorizations, shipping-preparation steps, and transport-label artifacts
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, quarantine records, and change-control documentation
technician notes, certified-dealer or training evidence, customer communications, and closure acknowledgements
systems_of_record: NHTSA VIN, recall, and manufacturer-communications records
parts and electronic parts catalog systems
battery service center or return-management systems

allowed: classify EV service or warranty work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, vehicle identity, diagnostic evidence, warranty context, and source records needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final engineering diagnosis or battery design decisions
supplier approval, reman or substitution approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
formal safety release, recall-command, or dangerous-goods legal interpretation
goodwill reimbursement, buyback, lemon-law, or other financial or legal remedy authority
refuse: continue EV service when VIN, warranty, recall, or high-voltage safety evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark EV work complete without post-repair validation, claim or completion evidence, and required battery return artifacts
bypass traceability, release, recall, certified-repair, or quality controls to protect throughput or customer sentiment

domain_failure_modes: the wrong vehicle or battery is serviced because VIN decode, OEM portal, and repair-order identity were not reconciled
an EV case advances without active diagnostic evidence or certified-repair prerequisites
open safety campaigns or damaged-battery indicators are treated as normal warranty work instead of a hold or escalation condition
replacement battery, charger, or related component traceability is lost across parts ordering, installation, and return handling
a removed battery is released or shipped without the required packaging, labeling, or routing evidence
supplier substitutions or reman decisions are treated as local service choices instead of procurement and supplier-quality escalations
the lane drifts into engineering, quality release, dangerous-goods legal interpretation, or financial concession authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: OEM-private portal rules and labor-operation coding
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public NHTSA and OEM sources establish the validated baseline, but each tenant still needs its private OEM portal entitlements, labor-operation matrix, and dealer policy overlays retrieved at runtime for case-level execution.

Public sources establish that dangerous-goods and safety-release obligations matter, but carrier, state, and local legal requirements and lemon-law or buyback rules remain tenant- and jurisdiction-specific and must be escalated rather than guessed.

notes: Authoritative EV case records start with VIN-grounded vehicle identity, open recall and manufacturer communication status, OEM warranty or campaign applicability, diagnostic evidence, battery or component traceability, and documented completion or return artifacts.

The lane stops before final engineering diagnosis, supplier approval, formal safety or logistics release authority, dangerous-goods legal interpretation, and financial or legal remedy decisions even when the dealer queue is under pressure.
